title: Bug Fix — Cache Invalidation Race Condition
description: A race condition in the product cache allowed stale data to be served after an update. The fix adds a write-through invalidation step inside the DB transaction.

nodes.label: update_product()
nodes.description: Updates a product record. Previously invalidated the cache AFTER committing, creating a window where another request could repopulate the cache with stale data. Fixed by invalidating inside the transaction.
nodes.signature: def update_product(db: Session, product_id: int, data: ProductUpdate) -> Product

nodes.label: get_product()
nodes.description: Cache-aside read. Checks cache first; on miss, loads from DB and populates cache. Unchanged.
nodes.signature: def get_product(db: Session, product_id: int) -> Product

nodes.label: cache.delete()
nodes.description: Redis DEL call. Fast synchronous operation.

nodes.label: DB Session
nodes.description: SQLAlchemy session. db.commit() flushes and ends the transaction.

nodes.label: Cache
nodes.description: Product cache. Keys are 'product:{id}', TTL 5 minutes.

nodes.label: product_router.py
nodes.description: FastAPI router — calls update_product on PATCH /products/{id}. Unchanged.

steps.label: load from DB → stale repopulates cache
steps.example: {"id": 42, "name": "Widget", "price": 9.99}  // old price, written back to cache
